perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to keep confidential or protect such Proprietary Information as it would its own confidential and proprietary information at the highest level of proprietary material.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to keep confidential or protect such Proprietary Information as it deems appropriate, without the obligation to treat it with the same level of care as its own confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the Recipient can handle the Proprietary Information as they deem appropriate. This contradicts Section 3's original intention to protect the Proprietary Information at the 'highest level of proprietary material.' Now, the level of protection is ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees to keep confidential or protect such Proprietary Information as it deems appropriate, without the obligation to treat it with the same level of care as its own confidential information. Disclosure of such Proprietary Information shall be restricted to those Representatives of Recipient who (i) have a need to know the information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Business Arrangement between the parties and (ii) are informed by the Disclosing Party of the confidential and proprietary nature of the Proprietary Information. The Disclosing Party will cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement and the Disclosing Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the Recipient can handle Proprietary Information as they deem appropriate, removing the obligation to treat it with the same level of care as their own confidential information. This directly contradicts the original intention to protect the Proprietary Information at the 'highest level of proprietary material,' as indicated in the perturbation explanation. The contradicted text repeats this new condition and continues to describe further obligations. This modification fundamentally alters the level of protection required, creating a contradiction because the agreement no longer explicitly mandates the 'highest level' of protection and introduces ambiguity. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Proprietary Information, including all applicable intellectual property rights residing in the Proprietary Information (including without limitation, patents, copyrights, industrial designs, trademarks and trade secrets), unless otherwise specified in writing by Disclosing Party, are and shall remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, including any and all reproductions in any form of said Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All Proprietary Information, including all applicable intellectual property rights residing in the Proprietary Information (including without limitation, patents, copyrights, industrial designs, trademarks and trade secrets), shall become the property of the Recipient upon termination of this agreement. All reproductions in any form of Proprietary Information will also be transferred to the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that all Proprietary Information remains the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. This change directly contradicts that by stating that the Proprietary Information transfers to the Recipient upon termination. This creates a conflict over ownership and usage rights after the agreement ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Proprietary Information, including all applicable intellectual property rights residing in the Proprietary Information (including without limitation, patents, copyrights, industrial designs, trademarks and trade secrets), shall become the property of the Recipient upon termination of this agreement. All reproductions in any form of Proprietary Information will also be transferred to the Recipient. All such Proprietary Information (whether prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives on its behalf) shall be returned or destroyed, at the election of Disclosing Party or upon request of Disclosing Party provided, however, that all notes, analyses, conditions, studies, interpretations, memoranda or other documents, to the extent that they contain, reflect or are based upon the Proprietary Information, may be destroyed (rather than delivered); provided further that Board or Board Committee minutes need neither be delivered nor destroyed. Upon the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall execute a certificate of destruction of such Proprietary Information when destroyed by Recipient, whether intentionally or accidentally, and transmit the same to Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text directly contradicts the Contradicted Text and the Original Text. The Original Text states that all Proprietary Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party. However, the Changed Text states that upon termination, the Proprietary Information transfers to the Recipient. The Contradicted Text also states that all Proprietary Information shall become the property of the Recipient upon termination of this agreement, but then stipulates that this information must be returned or destroyed. This creates a direct conflict, as the Recipient cannot both own and be required to destroy the same information. This fits the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that Recipient violates this Agreement or Disclosing Party has reasonable cause to feel that its Proprietary Information is in danger of being disclosed or misused, either Party may at any time, in addition to all other recourse under law, terminate this Agreement and demand and be entitled to the return from Recipient of all Proprietary Information in all existing forms.
perturbation.changed_text: Termination of this Agreement is solely at the discretion of the Recipient, regardless of any violation or potential misuse of Proprietary Information. The Disclosing Party has no right to terminate.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows either party to terminate the agreement if the recipient violates it or if the Disclosing Party fears its Proprietary Information is in danger. The change gives the Recipient sole discretion to terminate, removing the Disclosing Party's right, and contradicts the original balanced termination conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination of this Agreement is solely at the discretion of the Recipient, regardless of any violation or potential misuse of Proprietary Information. The Disclosing Party has no right to terminate. 9. It is further understood and agreed that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by Recipient and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctions and orders for specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows either party to terminate the agreement under certain conditions. The changed text gives the Recipient sole discretion to terminate and explicitly denies the Disclosing Party the right to terminate. Since the contradicted text states the same thing as the changed text, but based on the explanation we know it is an introduced contradiction, this creates a clear and direct conflict, fulfilling the criteria for a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', specifically by changing the termination conditions and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: The provisions of this Agreement pertaining to Proprietary Information disclosed under this Agreement shall continue and survive for a period of three (3) years after the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions of this Agreement pertaining to Proprietary Information expire immediately upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified that the provisions relating to Proprietary Information survive for three years after termination. The change states that those provisions expire immediately upon termination, creating a conflict on how long confidentiality obligations last.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The provisions of this Agreement pertaining to Proprietary Information expire immediately upon termination of this Agreement. 4 <PAGE> 5 IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written. CANADIAN NATIONAL RAILWAY COMPANY BY: /s/ Jean Pierre Ouellet ITS: Chief Legal Officer & Corporate Secretary ILLINOIS CENTRAL CORP. BY: ITS: 5
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that provisions pertaining to Proprietary Information survive for three years after termination. The changed text states that these provisions expire immediately upon termination. The contradicted text also states that the provisions expire immediately upon termination. Since the changed text directly contradicts the original agreement and the contradicted text, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
